responses.text: The pandemic disproportionately affects the psychological health of children, adolescents and women
The leading cause of death among young people in Japan is suicide
Japan will introduce the position of Minister of loneliness due to COVID-19.
During the pandemic, people who were 95% close to suicide faced new challenges.
The number of suicides in Japan fell by 14% during the first wave of the pandemic in February-June 2020
The number of suicides has increased year on year, with particularly high rates among women and children.
The economic problems brought about by COVID-19 contributed to the increase in the number of suicides.
Suicide rates in Japan declined between 2009 and 2019, but the trend reversed after the outbreak of COVID-19.
The number of suicides in Japan reached 21,077 in 2020, the first increase in 11 years, and even 2.5 times the number of deaths from COVID-19.
The medical association and eight health institutions in Japan have declared a state of emergency
Especially housewives are affected by the pandemic
Japan has one of the highest suicide rates in the world, with 18.5 suicides per 100,000 people
The post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation was taken by Minister for Regional Development Tetsushi Sakamoto.
The increase in mental trauma is one of the reasons for suicide.
Japan ’ s October suicides are “more than all Coronavirus deaths this year”
The number of suicides under the age of 20 was 659, an increase of 10.0% year-on-year, the highest since 2000.
A long-term economic downturn caused by the epidemic could lead to a rebound in the suicide rate
Japan's suicide rate fell below 20,000 for the first time in 40 years.
The number of suicides in Japan increased by 21.3% in October 2020, with a significant increase of 82.6% among women.
Japanese Prime Minister Yoshihide Suga has rejected the declaration of a new state of emergency in Japan
The epidemic has exacerbated women's worries about work, childcare, care and domestic violence, according to the Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare.
The mortality rate of suicide in Japan is higher than in other G7 countries
The limitation of activity and the sense of loss of the meaning of existence have crushed many people with the pain of unseen existence.
Suicide rates increased by 37% among women and by 49% among children and adolescents
Unemployment, social isolation and concern for the future are contributing to the rise in suicide cases in Japan.
The number of suicides in Japan increased by 16% in the summer and autumn of 2020.
The Japanese cabinet has set up an inter-departmental task force to understand the problem of loneliness and investigate its impact.
About 40% of suicide prevention organizations in Japan have temporarily closed or reduced their working hours due to the epidemic
Suicide ranked first among causes of death among 25-29 year olds in Japan
The presence of loved ones and professional activities can have a protective effect on mental health.
The number of men in their 20s and 50s who committed suicide in August was 706, an increase of 6.6% over the same period last year.
20,919 Japanese committed suicide in 2020, an increase of 3.7 percent from the previous year.
The Palestinian Authority rejected the diplomatic actions of the Persian Gulf states vis-à-vis the Zionist regime
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas announced that the Palestinians would no longer abide by their agreements with Israel and Washington.

The Palestinian leadership has said it can no longer see the United States as a mediator in peace talks with the Israelis and will be forced to freeze recognition of Israel until it recognizes the State of Palestine.

Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called on the Palestinian Central Council to hold a meeting in the middle of next month to make appropriate decisions on relations with Israel and the United States.

Hamas spokesman Hazem Qasim called on the PA leaders to align themselves with other Palestinian national and Islamic groups and movements and to confront the growing dangers facing the Palestinian issue.

Two people were injured when a Palestinian rocket fired in protest against the normalization of relations between the United Arab Emirates, Bahrain and Israel, and Israel responded by bombing the Gaza Strip.
The deal of the century or the deal of stigma will go to hell.

The agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Israel is to eliminate the Palestinian issue, the issue of Jerusalem as the capital, the right to return of the Palestinian refugees and return to the 1967 borders.

Palestinian Authority Prime Minister Mohammed Ashtiyah pointed to the agreement between the United Arab Emirates and Israel to normalize relations and halt the plan for the annexation of the Palestinian territories.

The Foreign Ministry of the Palestinian Authority also condemned the aggressive attack by the Zionist army on the leaders of the Islamic Jihad in Damascus and Gaza and called on the international community and the United Nations to act to stop the crimes of the regime.
Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas has said it no longer has a commitment to implement previous agreements with Israel and the United States, including those on security.
Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas called for an extraordinary meeting of the Arab League as well as an urgent meeting of Palestinian leaders and groups to discuss the consequences of the agreement.
Ismail Haniyeh, leader of the Palestinian Hamas group, called it a "declaration of war" against the Palestinians and called for a new "intifada" against Israel.

UN: Israeli annexation of Palestinian territories is a fatal blow to peace process

Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas said he had informed Egypt that the Palestinians would oppose the deal of the century and that security services would not be ordered to prevent demonstrators from reaching the call lines.

Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas will meet in Ramallah with local Palestinian factions to discuss the growing ties between Israel and Arab states and try to ‘ put an end to the differences ’ between rival groups.
Hamad al-Raqb, one of the leaders of the Hamas movement, said that compromise with the Zionist enemy has destroyed a large part of the Palestinian forces.

Palestinian media said that the Israeli army forces brutally suppressed the protesters by attacking peaceful Palestinian demonstrations against the Israeli settlement in the al-Ras area in the west of the Salafite province in the West Bank.
The plan to annex part of the West Bank by Israel is inconsistent with the Oslo Accords.

Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas said Israel does not respect any of its agreements with the Palestinians and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu does not want peace.
Ismail Haniyeh, head of Hamas' political bureau, said: "We have difficult problems related to our internal and regional situation, and there is no other way but to achieve Palestinian unity."

International news agencies reported that on Friday, on the first day of a six-week Palestinian demonstration along the border of the Gaza Strip with Israel, seven Palestinians were killed by Israeli forces and a number of others were injured.
The resolution calls for an end to Israeli settlements in the Palestinian territories.
The people of Palestine are fatigued under Israeli occupation
Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas has said he will no longer accept U.S. plans for a Middle East peace process.
Hamas leader Ismail Haniyeh called on Palestinian Authority President Mahmoud Abbas to end peace talks with Israel.
The Palestinian resistance launched rocket fire from the occupied territories in response to the Zionist military attack in occupied Jerusalem and the worshippers.

A national decision has been taken at different levels of the Executive Committee, the Fatah Central Committee and the Palestinian leadership, according to which the plan to annex parts of the West Bank to the occupied territories is "rejected".
More than 2 million foreign patients have come to Korea for medical treatment
Correcting complications from unauthorized cosmetic surgeries can be costly and time-consuming.
The shortage of professional doctors in China's medical beauty industry has produced many disguised solutions, such as letting consultants act as "consulting doctors".

The proportion of mainland tourists consulting for hair transplant has begun to rise recently.
Excessive overtaking of cosmetic surgeries has occurred even during the Coronavirus pandemic.
People are looking to turn the Coronavirus era into a golden age of reaching their ideal face.
Most medical institutions in the US are private and set tariffs themselves.
Nose surgery and Botox injections are at the top of beauty operations
Wang Mouhua had only trained in Guangzhou and obtained the qualification of a senior esthetician, not qualified to practice medicine.
In the past two years, the mainland has attracted a lot of Korean and Japanese people to do plastic surgery.
These tourists refer to West Azerbaijan to benefit from medical services and surgeries of special diseases, as well as cosmetic surgeries like hair transplant.
Medical tourism will attract people from neighbouring regions such as the Mainland and Macau to participate.
Statistics of cosmetic surgery have been growing not only in Iran, but also in many countries of the world.

Liposuction surgery may also have many complications.
Restrictions imposed due to coronavirus have been considered in hospitals, but many beauty clinics underground and perhaps in unsanitary conditions, carry out operations.
More and more Koreans, Japanese, and foreign students are going to the mainland for plastic surgery.
Medical tourism in most cases includes dental services, cosmetic surgery and other types of surgeries
Some clinics around the world say the number of cosmetic surgeries has increased during the coronavirus era.
Photos on social media and the appearance of celebrities on some reality TV shows are among the reasons men want to change their bodies.
37.5% of foreign patients go to cosmetic surgery and dermatology hospitals
Surgery to get large buttocks is not a low-risk procedure.
The staff of the bureau said that the cosmetic hospital involved in the incident has the relevant qualifications and the medical paperwork is standardized.
In South Korea, plastic surgery is as common as a haircut.
Medical tourism is an economic activity that requires trade in services and participation of at least two medical and tourism sectors
Cosmetic surgery in the United Kingdom is very popular, and the number of cosmetic surgeries has risen sharply.
Attention to appearance has increased in the last decade, with emphasis on being attractive by bloggers, celebrities, cinema, and media.
The government in India allowed private medical institutions to conduct tests and treat patients with COVID-19, allocating about 25% of places for them in large clinics.
Lipomodeling is also used to harmonize the face.
Some organizations and personnel without qualification can carry out related business, illegally practice medicine, or even "black clinics".
South Korea, Brazil, the United States, and Japan have 20, 14, 11, and 10 times the level of registered cosmetic doctors per million people compared to China.
A new branch of tourism called health tourism has emerged in Iran
Men are also sometimes candidates for cosmetic surgeries for various reasons
Men's demand for their own "face value" is increasing in the era of "small fresh meat" popularity.
Hsinchu Mayor Lin Chih-chien said that good parks improve the living environment.

New Taipei Civil Affairs Minister Ke Ching-chung said that in traditional urban areas with high population densities such as Xinbei Banqiao, Xinzhuang, Wugu, and Sanchong, most park facilities have reached the end of use.
The park will include children's play facilities and physical facilities for the elderly.
Another "pocket square" will be arranged in the courtyard of the house on Lyatoshinsky Street, 26-b in the Goloseevsky district of the capital.

The park features a stage for concerts, children's horizontal bars with an original design, hammocks for recreation, sports simulators, parking lots, and modern lighting.

Not every family lives near Xinsheng Park, and the park is located next to a railway track, making it unsafe for children.
City Councilman Yuen Cheng has sought to build a landscaping park that will integrate into the nearby community.

Qiaoren Park will provide a safe and convenient recreational space for the public and children.
They will restore playgrounds, install fences, equip parking pockets, repair areas for quiet rest and walking dogs, put lawns and flower beds in order, and plant trees and shrubs.
The Green Water Ying Park provides more natural sightseeing spots for the public.
Parks in cities provide biodiverse habitats to increase biodiversity and reduce urban heat island phenomena.
The project will be carried out by the way of the cultural elementary school and neighboring residents to participate.
Large parks in megacities are often far away and not always convenient to get to.

Chu Si-jung, mayor of Xinzhuang, pointed out that Jianfu Park and Shuangfeng Park originally had only a few traditional children's toys, which were less interesting, and the five senses of children were more single.

The neighborhood park of Shaghayegh is being built in an area of 18 thousand square meters and in four levels in Valiasr city.
Some of the areas needed to develop green space in dense tissues of the region in order to increase the per capita green space is on the agenda and will be operational this year.

Only a small proportion of park users do not wear masks, accounting for 22.7% and 1.2% in New York, and 0.7% in Philadelphia and 0.9% in New York are spitting or coughing without covering their mouths.
Turkey has a significant presence in Uzbekistan, Tajikistan, and Afghanistan, with Turkish language and literature taught in several universities.

The establishment of soft power in Turkey was not only the result of the efforts of a single organization, but also 20 governmental and non-governmental organizations.

The movies and series that are broadcast through Turkish satellite networks have more themes and content of betrayal, lies, revenge and so on.

Egypt opened the Berenice military base south of the Red Sea to counter Turkish threats and ambitions in the region.
Turkey’s geographical location has allowed it to introduce its culture to both the East and the West.

A test vehicle for extracting oxygen from the Martian atmosphere, as well as a "space helicopter" that is set to fly for the first time on a planet other than Earth, are among those on the Perseverance rover.
Perseverance has an experimental installation MOXIE to receive oxygen from carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of Mars.
This is the first time that humans have used instruments in outer space to convert other gases into oxygen.
Musk has a bold idea for solving the Martian atmosphere: detonating a nuclear weapon at the north and south poles of Mars
Mars' atmosphere contains 95% carbon dioxide, 2.6% nitrogen, 1.9% argon, 0.16% oxygen and 0.06% carbon monoxide.
MOXIE was first tested on April 20 and produced more than 5 grams of oxygen for an hour of operation
Cyanobacteria can use scarce water in the Martian soil for photosynthesis and produce by-products to feed other microbes.
Giant mirrors can be hung over the poles of Mars to reflect sunlight and melt glaciers, releasing carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.
NASA is developing a rocket to take people to Mars and is experimenting with producing oxygen on the planet.
NASA plans to send humans to Mars in the 2030s.
Atmospheric pressure and temperature on Mars change along with the oxygen content.
MOXIE converts carbon dioxide into oxygen and carbon monoxide (CO)

A new experiment conducted by researchers at the University of Bremen in Germany has shown for the first time that a type of bacteria called Anabaena cyanobacteria, or blue and green algae, can thrive in Martian atmospheric conditions, allowing it to survive on Mars.
The use of known carbon dioxide on Mars to achieve the transformation of the earth is probably not enough with existing human technology.
Oxygen production on Mars is a prerequisite for manned missions.
Lichens and cyanobacteria can be used to start the process of photosynthesis on Mars and create oxygen.
The idea involves deploying an electromagnetic shield between Mars and the Sun to protect the Red Planet from the solar wind.
Cyanobacteria can use the gases present in the Martian atmosphere at low overall pressure as a source of carbon and nitrogen.
Bayer acquired Monsanto for about $63 billion in 2018 and has taken on tens of thousands of lawsuits related to Roundup.
Monsanto has been sued in the United States for suspected cancer caused by the herbicide Roundup, and Bayer is seeking to settle the lawsuit with $11 billion in damages.

Bayer AG will announce that it has reached an approximately $10.5 billion agreement with plaintiffs in the United States who have accused its Roundup herbicide of causing cancer to settle tens of thousands of lawsuits.
A jury awarded $80 million in damages to a Californian who claimed Roundup caused him cancer.
The agreement was made with an estimated 50,000 to 85,000 plaintiffs.
German pharmaceutical company Bayer said its U.S. company Monsanto production of herbicide "Roundup" reported cases of cancer in the United States in the second quarter of about 5,000 new cases.
Bayer, the German chemical company, said on Wednesday that a legal dispute over cancer caused by its Roundup brand herbicide has reached a $2 billion settlement with regulators.
A lawsuit in 2019 found that a Roundup user, Edwin Hardeman, received $5.3 million in damages from Monsanto for cancer.
Bayer has settled 88,500 of 125,000 claims.
Bayer announced plans in June to settle all legal lawsuits against Monsanto for $12 billion.
In August 2018, a California court jury found another California man was awarded $289 million in damages for his cancer caused by Roundup.
1,861 of the 3,787 plaintiffs in the lawsuit remain unsettled.
A court in the United States ruled that Bayer should pay $289.2 million to school gardener Diwayne Johnson, who contracted cancer as a result of the use of the herbicide Roundup.
The claimants have not yet formally agreed to accept this compensation
A court in the United States halved compensation for cancer patients from the herbicide Roundup
The compensation case cannot end all legal disputes, but 75% of the litigation will be agreed through the terms of the settlement.

The California Supreme Court declined to hear an appeal by Bayer AG, which sought to overturn a jury ’ s ruling that the company ’ s Roundup herbicide caused a gardener to develop cancer, leaving the company with few other options to avoid paying the $20.4 million verdict.
Bayer will pay about $9.5 billion to settle claims filed by lawyers representing some 95,000 plaintiffs.
An agreement to settle a large number of lawsuits was terminated earlier this month.
Bayer offered to pay up to $8 billion to settle numerous lawsuits in the United States.

Italian prosecutor Ornella Chicca said the actual painting was verified as authentic, but it remains unclear whether the painting was later smuggled back into the wall mezzanine or was hidden there from the beginning, pending further examination and clarification.
Employees of the gallery identified it as "Portrait of a woman" Klimt and examined the seals and stamps on the back of the canvas.
The painting found is indeed the “Portrait of a Lady ” by Gustav Klimt, which was stolen from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Modern Art in 1997.
Gardeners found the painting embedded in one of the garden walls while cleaning the gallery's garden ivy.
The painting “Portrait of a woman ” by Gustav Klimt is worth 60 million euros.
A painting by well-known Austrian painter Klimt was stolen in an Italian art museum nearly 23 years ago.
The painting was stolen on February 22, 1997 from the Ricci Oddi Gallery of Contemporary Art as preparations for the Klimt exhibition were underway.
The painting was painted by Klimt in 1916-1917.
Prosecutor Ornella Chicca confirmed that the painting is real using infrared radiation and other non-invasive detection methods.
Upon the return of the painting, the gallery ’ s director, Massimo Ferrari, referred to it as part of his gallery identity.

In the Italian city of Piacenza, a gardener peeled ivy off the wall in the Ricci Oddi gallery and accidentally found a painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman ” stolen 22 years ago from the same gallery.
A gardener was cleaning the ivy of the museum’s outer wall when he found a metal plate on the wall, and when he opened it, he found a canvas painting in a black sackcloth bag.
Gustav Klimt's 1912 portrait of Austrian symbolist painter Adele Bloch Bauer was sold for $87 million at Christie's in New York.
According to the director of the gallery, there is every reason to believe in the authenticity of the picture

Thieves of a stolen painting by Gustav Klimt, which was recently discovered in the same Italian gallery where it was stolen after 22 years, have admitted to returning the artwork to the scene of the theft.
The president of the gallery of contemporary art Massimo Ferrari said the picture is too well preserved, especially when it comes to small scratches and a back with traces of wax.

Two men admitted that 22 years ago they stole and then returned the painting by Gustav Klimt “Portrait of a Woman” and not all the time kept it in a niche in the wall of the Ricci Oddi gallery in Piacenza, Italy, where it was found by gardeners.
The painting features a special symbolic pattern.
The thieves said in a letter to Ermanno Marian, an employee of the newspaper Libertà, that they stole the work of the Austrian artist worth 60 million euros in February 1997, and then returned it.
Massimo Ferrari, the museum’s curator, said that the stamp after the painting appeared to be genuine and related to Klimt’s other works.
French authorities have announced that the painting by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt will be returned to his legal heir because it was sold under Nazi Germany’s oppression.

Due to the closure of galleries and museums in Italy due to the spread of the coronavirus, it has not been possible for the public to visit the work in person, and officials of the Ricci Odi Gallery have provided the opportunity to visit the painting in its former exhibition.

French Culture Minister Roselyne Bachelot said that the return of the painting was a recognition of the suffering endured by people at the time, and the work itself was a testimony to the many shattered lives of the Nazi era.
Climate change has reduced rainfall.
Temperatures are rising both in winter and in summer.

Golestan Agriculture Organization's head of crisis management said that the reduction of rainfall and drought in the current agricultural year caused 277,000 hectares of agricultural land in the province between 10 and 100 percent damage.
Kaohsiung jade lotus bag lychee has lost harvest due to drought.
Climate change worsens the problem of water supply.
Adopting principled measures, such as the plan for the development of new irrigation and the change in the pattern of cultivation of agricultural and garden crops, should be considered.
Human activity influences precipitation intensity.
Rising CO2 levels are forcing the planet to change and humanity will face unprecedented climate conditions.
Warm winters will have an impact on crops.
The rainfall of Isfahan province decreased by 38% in the current agricultural year
The temperature has increased by more than 2.5 degrees Celsius.

The major damage to the agricultural sector in the cities of Konarak, Nikshahr, Chabahar, Qasrgand, Delegan, Mehrstan, soldier, bumpur, phenoj, apple and Soran, was Khash, according to preliminary estimates of damage groups to agricultural subsectors estimated 613 billion tomans.

Plans have been made to reduce the level of cultivation of high-water crops every year and replace them with other crops that have economic justification for farmers.

The abnormal climate has caused turbulence in the crop growth cycle, resulting in crop damage and poor harvests.

Climate warming makes summers longer and hotter.

Farmers should change their management of cultivation.
Lack of rainfall affects air pollution levels.

The winter has become milder, and the summer is now much hotter.
Rice and apple crops suffered more than 14,000 hectares of damage, about 10.6 billion yen.

Water restrictions exist in the region's agricultural sector.
Crops adapted to dry weather need to be applied in the agricultural sector.

The film “The Devil Does Not Exist” recounts narratives about freedom under the pressure of coercion and deals with the death penalty in Iran.
The Golden Bear for Best Film of the 70th Berlinale goes to "Sheytan vojud nadarad" (There Is No Evil), directed by Mohammad Rasoulof.

The film won the Golden Bear for Best Film and the Silver Bear for Best Actor and Actress at the 61st Berlin International Film Festival.
Mohammad Rasoulof was sentenced to one year in prison and two years ban from leaving the country in late July 2019 on charges of “propaganda against the Islamic system”.

French authorities intercepted 19 migrants trying to sneak into Britain in the English Channel yesterday night.
Home Secretary Sajid Javid declared the situation a major event on Friday and asked for a dialogue with the French side to work closely on the issue.
An Iranian refugee waiting in France to enter Britain reported that four members of an Iranian family of five died in the English Channel, and the body of their 15-month-old baby has not been found.
The Calais refugee camp is home to illegal migrants who cross the sea from Africa and the Middle East to Europe, concentrated in Calais, waiting for a chance to travel to the UK.
Britain and France have signed a new agreement to stop illegal migrants from venturing across the English Channel by stepping up patrols and deploying technology on French beaches.
